mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns are not significantly positive during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within three months signals imminent labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO trends yield negative or flat 20-day SPX returns during expansion regimes, with t-stat < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation surging above 3.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 70% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market that sustains consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth vs 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and real personal consumption expenditures signals weakening consumer demand relative to overall economic output, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence metric shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) for 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.8% signals a sudden supply-side contraction that disrupts earnings expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the sharp industrial production drop.
